mnemonics: LIZN: Large samples use Z-tests, Normal distribution
keyPoints: A large sample size (typically n > 30) allows the use of the Z-test and the assumption of a normal sampling distribution.
The test statistic is calculated as Z = (sample_statistic - hypothesized_parameter) / standard_error.
The conclusion is based on comparing the calculated Z-value to critical values from the standard normal distribution.
commonMistakes: Using a Z-test for a small sample size (n < 30) without a normal population distribution.
Confusing the standard deviation of the population (σ) with the standard error (σ/√n) in the denominator of the test statistic.
Misinterpreting the p-value as the probability that the null hypothesis is true, rather than the probability of observing the data given that the null is true.
